Identity.History: Shapur I ruled the Sasanian Empire from 240–270 AD, first as co-ruler with his father Ardashir I and then as shahanshah. He expanded Persian power across Mesopotamia, Armenia, and the eastern frontiers, but is best remembered for his victories over Rome. Shapur defeated several Roman emperors and famously captured Emperor Valerian at Edessa in 260 AD, a humiliation immortalized in Sasanian rock reliefs. He founded cities, patronized religious communities, and strengthened the empire’s prestige, making Sasanian Persia Rome’s great eastern rival.
